generation_prompt: Given the enriched persona from Step 2 (interview transcript and extracted profile) and the expert views from Step 2b, generate a comprehensive preference profile covering all major life domains. Each preference must cite interview evidence.

health_and_wellness.summary: Active runner managing borderline hypertension through exercise and diet; hereditary shellfish sensitivity
details: Runs 3-5 miles along Lake Mendota 3x/week (MWF at 6:15am) with Madison Runners Club
Borderline hypertension — cutting sodium, monitoring BP, doctor recheck in 6 months
Shellfish sensitivity (hereditary from mother) — avoids shrimp/crab, double-checks menus
Tracks meals in bullet journal for sodium awareness
Meal preps to avoid eating fast food at work
Takes Benadryl as backup for accidental shellfish exposure
health_and_wellness.evidence: Q6: 'blood pressure... a little elevated'; Q7: 'Dana will literally text me if I don't show up'; Q8: 'bad reaction at a restaurant about a year ago'

food.summary: Midwestern-Italian fusion home cook with specific signature dishes and farm-to-table leanings
details: Signature dishes: cheddar-crusted bratwurst, cheese-curd poutine, spiced apple crumble
Italian-American family recipes: homemade pasta, Sunday gravy (Nonna's tradition)
Grows tomatoes, basil, and peppers in raised-bed garden
Buys seasonal produce and cheese curds at Saturday farmers market
Avoids shellfish (shrimp, crab) due to sensitivity
Trying to reduce sodium intake for BP management
Comfort baking: spiced apple crumble is go-to stress food
food.evidence: Q1: 'cheddar-crusted bratwurst next to a plate of rigatoni'; Q10 follow-up: 'spiced apple crumble is my go-to comfort bake'; Q7: 'cheese curds, bratwurst, all of it'

travel.summary: Budget-conscious regional traveler with aspirational international dreams
details: Regular: Door County, WI weekend getaways with Dana (hiking, cheese curds, wineries)
Aspirational: Napa Valley, CA (wine country and culinary tourism)
Dream: Kyoto, Japan (someday trip)
Occasional: Milwaukee, WI for Brewers games with Jess
Constraint: Must balance travel costs with diner savings fund
travel.evidence: Q4: 'We've gone to Door County together a couple times'; Q10: 'I keep looking at pictures of Napa Valley and Kyoto'

social_and_relationships.summary: Family-centered with a tight inner circle of 5; values loyalty and mutual support
details: Mom (Lucia) is most important person — sees 2x/week, daily-ish contact
Nonna — visits Wednesdays, origin of cooking tradition and Italian identity
Dana — running partner 3x/week, mentor-friend, Door County trips
Jess — coworker confidante, shift-swap partner, Brewers buddy
Tony — younger brother, protective older sister role, weekly contact
Values loyalty — Kaylee friendship died after trust-breaking promotion
Community connections: Marcus (cheese vendor), parish community, running club
social_and_relationships.evidence: Q2: 'My mom is probably the most important person'; Q4: 'Dana... She's become like a real friend'; Q2: 'Jess is the one person I can actually vent to'

home_and_lifestyle.summary: Routine-obsessed bullet journaler in a modest Madison apartment with a raised-bed garden
details: Bullet journal tracks spending, meals, shifts, savings, running, and weekly routines
Morning routine mapped to the minute — disruption causes anxiety
Raised-bed vegetable garden (tomatoes, basil, peppers)
Meal preps on Sundays as part of weekly reset
Budget-conscious: clips coupons, logs every receipt, $150/month leisure limit
Lives in Madison, WI (likely apartment with garden access)
home_and_lifestyle.evidence: Q1: 'I started a bullet journal a couple years ago to keep my head straight'; Q5 follow-up: 'Now if my routine gets disrupted I get kind of anxious'

entertainment.summary: Indie film, folk music, and low-key social gatherings
details: Wes Anderson films — attends indie theater screenings (Rushmore, Royal Tenenbaums)
Self-help books (habit stacking, productivity)
Sudoku puzzles (stress relief, anxiety management)
Craft-brew tastings at local Madison breweries
entertainment.evidence: Q11: 'I went to a Wes Anderson double feature at the indie theater downtown'; Q10 follow-up: 'I'll do Sudoku... the focus it takes kind of shuts off the anxious part of my brain'

hobbies.summary: Running, cooking/baking, gardening, bullet journaling, Sudoku, craft beer
details: Running: 3-5 miles 3x/week, Lake Mendota, Madison Runners Club
Cooking/Baking: Italian-American and Midwestern recipes, hosts dinner gatherings
Raised-bed gardening: tomatoes, basil, peppers (spring/summer/fall)
Bullet journaling: daily habit, tracks all aspects of life
Sudoku: competitive edge outlet, anxiety management
Craft-brew tastings: local Madison breweries, occasional indulgence
Reading: self-help and productivity books
hobbies.evidence: Q1: 'I run three to five miles along Lake Mendota'; Q1: 'I have a raised bed out back'; Q5: 'maybe do some Sudoku'

work_and_career.summary: Ambitious food service worker with a clear upward trajectory toward diner ownership
details: Current: fast food/counter worker, 5 years at same chain, performing supervisor duties without title
Next step: shift supervisor promotion (actively pursuing, passed over once for Kaylee)
Medium-term: restaurant manager, community college hospitality program, ServSafe certification
Long-term dream: own a neighborhood diner ('a real neighborhood place where people come for breakfast')
Saves ~$100/month in 'diner fund'
Skills: POS, cash handling, food safety, inventory, team scheduling, shift leadership
work_and_career.evidence: Q1: 'My big dream is to own my own diner someday'; Q10: 'I've been channeling it into my diner savings and my community college research'

arts_and_culture.summary: Folk music, indie film, local arts scene
details: Music: John Prine (lyrical storytelling), Bon Iver (atmospheric indie folk)
Film: Wes Anderson (attends indie theater screenings)
Local arts: art walks and museum exhibits (from seed data)
Italian-American cultural traditions: Sunday dinners, Feast of Seven Fishes, homemade pasta
arts_and_culture.evidence: Q10 follow-up: 'I put on John Prine or Bon Iver and just go'; Q11: 'Wes Anderson double feature at the indie theater'

sports.summary: Dedicated Packers fan, casual Brewers follower, active runner
details: Green Bay Packers: watches every Sunday with family in fall, emotional investment
Milwaukee Brewers: summer viewer, occasional road trips to Milwaukee with Jess
Wisconsin Badgers basketball: fan (from seed data, absent from interview)
Madison Runners Club: active member, 3-5 miles 3x/week at Lake Mendota
Considering first half-marathon (Dana encouraging, Madison Mini in May)
sports.evidence: Q1 follow-up: 'If the Packers are on, my dad has the TV going'; Q5: 'catch a Brewers game'; Q2: 'Dana... she's done a bunch of half-marathons'

nature_and_outdoors.summary: Lake Mendota runner, raised-bed gardener, Door County hiker
details: Running along Lake Mendota path 3x/week
Raised-bed vegetable gardening (spring-fall)
Door County weekend getaways — hiking, lakeside relaxation
Farmers market every Saturday (outdoor in summer, indoor in winter)
Dreams of Kyoto — interest in nature/culture travel
nature_and_outdoors.evidence: Q1: 'I run three to five miles along Lake Mendota'; Q4: 'Door County... hiking, eating cheese curds, hitting up wineries'

technology.summary: Low-tech, analog-leaning — bullet journal over apps, basic tech user
details: Uses bullet journal (analog) for all tracking rather than apps
Basic phone usage — texting, probably social media
No technology hobbies or interests mentioned
Works with POS systems at restaurant (job skill, not hobby)
technology.evidence: Q1: 'I started a bullet journal a couple years ago'; Q11 follow-up: 'Every receipt gets logged' (in journal, not app)

society.summary: Community-oriented, values local connections and neighborhood identity
details: Values farmers market as weekly community ritual
Parish community involvement (potlucks, fish fry volunteering)
Running club as social community
Dreams of a neighborhood diner — community-building aspiration
Moderate political leaning, not strongly partisan
society.evidence: Q4: Marcus 'It's one of those community connections that makes Madison feel like home'; Q1: 'a real neighborhood place where people come for breakfast and know your name'

music.summary: Folk and indie folk listener, uses music for emotional regulation
details: John Prine — lyrical storytelling, listens while running and when stressed
Bon Iver — atmospheric indie folk, running and stress music
Classic rock — listed in seed data but not substantiated in interview
music.evidence: Q10 follow-up: 'I put on John Prine or Bon Iver and just go'

details: Running gear: $120 running shoes noted as significant expense
Work uniform/practical clothing for food service
No fashion interests or clothing brands mentioned
Budget-conscious — unlikely to splurge on fashion
fashion.evidence: Q11: 'I had to get new running shoes last month, which was like a hundred and twenty bucks, and that hurt'

verification_notes.seed_alignment: 13 of 15 preference categories have direct seed data alignment. Technology and Fashion are inferred from interview absence and financial constraints.
verification_notes.interview_coverage: All preferences cite specific interview quotes. Classic rock (Music) and Art walks (Arts & Culture) are carried from seed but lack interview substantiation — flagged in Step 2b verification.
verification_notes.expert_alignment: Preferences are consistent with all 4 expert views. Behavioral Economist confirms financial constraints shape entertainment, travel, and food choices.
